title: Response Commitment, Problem Details, Streaming, and Downloads · Contract Lens: Stream Backpressure Pipeline
summary: Handlers write success before work completes, expose stack traces, send inconsistent problem objects, ignore stream backpressure, and assume errors can replace committed headers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts stream backpressure pipeline increment in single-owner response and bounded stream protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use stream pipeline, write backpressure, bounded buffers, cancellation, error ownership, trailers, and finish-versus-close evidence for large responses.
Control JSON nullability, undefined omission, BigInt, dates, non-finite numbers, property order, cycles, and serialization failure before commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Stream Backpressure Pipeline · Theory Stream Backpressure Pipeline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8436 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose one response shape and stop a producer at the stream backpressure boundary; reject a replay before any external effect; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use stream pipeline, write backpressure, bounded buffers, cancellation, error ownership, trailers, and finish-versus-close evidence for large responses.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Stream Backpressure Pipeline contract for the supplied data.
